title: achras guatemalensis
intro: Achras guatemalensis, also known as the Sapote tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Sapote tree is valued for its edible fruit and medicinal properties, with the inner bark and roots being used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Achras guatemalensis are attributed to its rich content of saponins, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of Achras guatemalensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being, as well as to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
